If a snake is seen in a temple: the offerings of that temple will continue for a long time. If a snake keeps scaring (people) in a temple: angry gods will return to the country. If a snake is seen in the temple of Ištar: there will be a food-offering of Ištar. If a snake [...] in the temple of Ištar: that temple [...]. ruling These are the pertinent omens.

When I was sent to the Inner City, I made my observations, came back and report[ed] to the king: "The loin is not being offered to Aššur." The king, my lord, got angry and said: "They should resume offering it." Last year I wrote to the king, [my] lord, and told (them): "The king, my lord, gave me the order (personally)." Afterwards they wrote to the Go[ver]nor: "Write down the words of Akkull[anu] and those (with him); they are lyi[ng]." [The k]ing, my lord, did not decide (the matter). [What is it] that the king says now? [To]night the moon was surrounded by a halo, and the Ol[d Man] Star and Gemini sto[od] in it. ruling [If] the moon rid[es] a chariot in month Sililiti: the dominion of the king of Akkad will prosper, and he w[ill capture] his enemies. ruling — The moon is surrounded by a halo in Shebat (XI) and the Old Man Star stands in it. ruling If the moon is surrounded by a halo and the Old Man Star stands in it: a reign of long duration. ruling [If Ge]mini stand [in the halo of the m]oon and [......]. ruling (Break) May [the kin]g, [my lord], and [the peo]ple with him live fo[rever!] [From] Akkullanu.

The 10th day: at court, favorable. The 12th day: in the street, favorable. The 15th day: perfect seed. The 16th day: joy of heart. The 18th day: convert the cleaned (barley). The 20th day: let him kill a snake: he will reach the first rank. The 22nd day: at court, favorable; (good for) undertaking of an enterprise. The 24th day: joy of heart. The 26th day: (good) news. The 27th day: completely favorable. The 28th day: let him kill a snake. The 30th day: a good omen. ruling From Adad-šumu-uṣur.

(As to) what the king, my lord wrote to me: 'The clouds were dense, how did you observe that the gods saw each other (in opposition)?' — (the clouds) dispersed before daybreak; when he whom the king, my lord, knows revealed himself, we saw where the moon was standing. It amounts to an actual observation. Now, does not the king, my lord, (indeed) hear that they saw each other on the 14th?
[On the ...th of Nisan (I) the day] and the night were in balance: 6 'double-hours' of daylight, 6 'double-hours' of night. May Nabû (and) [Marduk] bless the king, our lord!
If the moon is surrounded by a halo, and two stars stand in the halo with the moon: a reign of long days. From Nabû-ahhe-iddin.

If the moon becomes visible on the 1st day: reliable speech; the land will become hap[py]. If the day reaches its normal length: a reign of long days. If the moon at its appearance we[ars] a crown: the king will reach the highest rank. ruling Mercury became visible in the east. If Scorpius is black: therein will be reconciliation and peace in the land. [If a planet] becomes visible within a month: rain and flood. ruling [If a] fog rolls [in Kislev (IX): severity of] cold spells. [Cold spells (mean)] cold. [If] there is continually fog [in the land: the dynasty of the land] will rule [the world]. From Bulluṭu.
If the moon becomes visible on the 1st day: reliable speech; the land will become happy. If the day reaches its normal length: a reign of long days. From Bulluṭu. (SPACER)
[If the moon] becomes visible [on the 1st day]: reliable speech; the land will become happy. If the day reaches its normal length: a reign of long days. (SPACER) From Bulluṭu. (SPACER)
If the moon becomes visible on the 1st day: reliable speech. If the day reaches its normal [length]: a reign of lo[ng] days. (SPACER) From Bu[lluṭu]. (SPACER)
If the moon [becomes visible] on the 1st day: reliable speech; [the land will become happy]. If the day [reaches] its normal length: a reign of [long] days. From Na[bû-mušeṣi]. (SPACER)
If the moon [becomes visible] on the 1st day: reli[able] speech; the land will become happy. If the day reaches its normal [length]: a reign of long days. From Nabû-mušeṣi. (SPACER)

If the moon becomes visible on the 1st day: reliable speech; the land will become happy. If the day reaches its normal length: a reign of long days. If the moon at its appearance wears a crown: the king will reach the highest rank. From Issar-šumu-ereš.

[If] the moon [becomes visible] on the 1st day: reliable speech; [the land will become] happy. ruling If the day reaches its normal [length]: a reign of lo[ng] days. ruling [The moon] will comp[lete ......] ... the 30th day. [If the moon becomes visible] on the 1st day: good for A[kkad], bad for Elam [and the Westland]. On the 14th day [it will be seen] with the sun. ruling From Balasî.

If the moon becomes visible on the 1st day: reliable speech; the land will become happy. ruling Of Nisan (I) (and) Tishri (VII). If (the moon) wears a crown: regularly the harvest of the land will prosper; the king will reach the highest rank. ruling (SPACER)

If the moon becomes visible on the 1st day: reliable speech; the land will become happy. If the day reaches its normal length: a reign of long days. Adar (XII) and Elul (VI) are beginning of the year, as Nisan (I) and Tishri (VII) are at the beginning of the year. The moon has sent a favorable omen on the length of the days of the reign to the king my lord. From Aššur-šarra[ni].

If the moon becomes visible on the 1st day: reliable speech, the land will become happy. If the day reaches its normal length: a reign of long days. ruling Twice or thrice we watched for Mars today (but) we did not see (it), it has set. Maybe the king my lord will say as follows: "Is there any (ominous) sign in (the fact) that it set?" (I answer): "There is not." From Issar-šumu-ereš.
(Beginning destroyed) [If the day] reaches its normal length: [a reign] of long days. ruling This night, [Mar]s came out [of] Cancer. [From] Issar-šumu-ereš. (SPACER)

If the moon at its appearance wears a crown: regularly [the harvest of the land will prosper]; the land will dwell as if in pastures near the city; the ki[ng will reach the highest rank]. If its horns [......] crown [......] crown [......]. [From] Bel-šuma-iškun.

If the moon be[comes visible] on the 1st day: reliable speech, the land will become happy. If the moon is bright at its appearance: the land Akkad will live in brightness; my troops will s[ee] plenty. If the moon's horns at its appearance are very point[ed]: the king of Akkad will rule the land wherever he int[ends]. If the day reaches [its] normal [length]: reigns of [long] days. If the days reach [their] normal [length]: the years of the king in [......], variant: [......]. ruling These omens are now very [appropriate] to ci[te] in that the first days [became long] after eac[h other] regularly. From Issar-šumu-e[reš].

[If the moon at its appearance] wears a crown: the harvest of the land will pro[sper; the land] will live [as if in pastures near the city]; the king [will reach] the highest rank. [If the moon's] horns are pointed [at its appearance]: business will ...[...]; there will b[e ......] in the land. [If the moon's] horns at its [appearance] are very pointed: the king of Akkad will weaken [the land wherever he turns]; wherever he turns [he will rule the land]. — It is seen [on the 1st day]: good for Akkad, bad for Elam. ruling [...... "to be p]ointed" (means) to be adorned, said of ho[rns]. ruling [Concerning] this order which [...-ap]lu-uṣur sent [to me] about [...], I shall [......] the king my lord. [...] I will accept [...] [...] ... [...] [...] many ... [...] [... the go]ds doing it, the king my lord [...] which I [...] to the king my lord [...] permanently [...] [...] upon me [...] [...] I fear the king: "[...]." [Perhaps the king my] lord will say: "[...]" — [...] I will sen[d ......]. ruling From Akkulla[nu].

If the day [reaches its normal length]: a reign of long [days]. ruling Normal length of a month (means) it comple[tes] the 30th day. ruling [If the moon's] horns at its appearance are very pointed: [the king of Akkad w]ill weaken [the land] wherever he turns; wherever he turns he will rule [the lan]d. ruling "To be pointed" means to be adorned, said of horns. ruling Tammuz (IV) means Subartu. ruling From Akkullanu.

(Beginning destroyed) [he will rule the land wherever he] turns. ruling "To be pointed" means to be [adorned, said of horns]. ruling If [... originates] in the lower part of the appearing [moon ...] the reign of the king will [be stable ...] much business [......]. ruling Mercury [is seen at the appearance of the moon]. ruling If the moon at its appearance [......]. If the moon becomes visible on the 1st day [......]. ruling [If on the] day of disappearance [......]. (Rest destroyed)

[If] the moon becomes visible on the 1st day: reli[able spe]ech; the land will become happy. [If the day] of disappearance of the moon reaches its normal [leng]th: the days of the ruler will be long. ruling (SPACER) (SPACER)

[If the moon at] its appearance [wears a crown: the king of the land will reach] the highest rank; the land wil[l dwell] as if in pastures near the city. It means that (the moon) will complete the day in Tishri (VII). ruling If the moon becomes visible on the 1st day: speech will become reliable; the land will become happy. ruling If the moon becomes visible on the 1st day: good for Akkad, bad for Elam [and] the Westland. ruling If the moon at its appearance [......]. (Rest destroyed)

[If the moon] at its appearance wears a crown: [the kin]g will reach the highest rank; [the land] will dwell as if in pastures near the city. ruling — [The moon] will wear a crown when it is seen. ruling If the moon's right horn at its appearance pierces the sky: there will be stable prices in the land; a revolt will be staged in the Westland. ruling "Its right horn pierces the sky," as it says, means it will slip into the sky and will not be seen; dir is "to slip," said of a horn. ruling At the appearance of the moon its right horn (signifies) the Westland. ruling If the moon becomes visible on the 1st day: good for Akkad, bad for Elam and the Westland. ruling The moon will be seen with the sun on the 14th day. ruling From Nabû-ahhe-eriba. ruling ruling
The moon will complete the day in Nisan (I). ruling (SPACER) (SPACER) From Nabû-ahhe-eriba.

(Beginning destroyed) [the moon will com]plete the day in Adar (XII); on the 14th day it will be see[n together] with (the sun). [The moon] will reject the day in Nisan (I); [the moon] will reject the day in Iyyar (II); [the moon] will be close to rejecting in Sivan (III). Much [... is] not before me. ruling [...] the king of kings [......] (Rest destroyed)
The moon will complete the day in Shebat (XI). ruling (SPACER) (SPACER) From Nabû-ahhe-eriba.

The m[oon] will complete [the day] on the ev[ening] of the beginning of the year: it is favorable fo[r the king] my lord. ruling Concerning Mercury, about which the king my lord wrote to me: yesterday Issar-šumu-ereš had an argument with Nabû-ahhe-eriba in the palace. Later, at night, they went and all made observations; they saw (it) and were satisfied. ruling From B[a]lasî.

If the moon [becomes visible] on the 1st day: reliable speech; the land will become happy. If the moon at [its] appea[rance] makes [...], and its dwelling is devastated: there will be [......], and [the son] will speak the [truth with his father]. ruling If [......]: the king of the Westland will rule the world. ruling If there exists in [......] stars: [......] will seek the moon. ruling If [......] (erased) If in Iyyar (II) the Pleiades, the Seven Gods, the great gods, rise at their appropriate time: the great gods will gather and make a favorable decision about the land. ruling In Iyyar (II) on the 1st day [the Breast of the Panther stands] in the middle of the sky opposite [your breast, and] the Pleiad[es ri]se.

[If the moon] becomes visible on the 28th [day]: good for Akkad, bad for E[lam]. ruling The moon st[ood there] on the 28th day. ruling This sign counterbalances the sign o[f the 12th day]. ruling When I wr[ote] to the king my lord, saying "the gods have opened the ears of the king [my lord]," (I meant) if something [happens] to [the king and] he worries, th[e gods ...] first send a message fr[om heaven] saying: ["Let him be on his guard", ......]. The king my lord [......].

If the moon at its appearance becomes visible on the 28th day as if on the 1st day: bad for the Westland. If the moon becomes visible on the 28th day: good for Akkad, bad for the Westland. (SPACER) From the Chief Scribe.

If the moon at its appearance is faint so that nobody can make it out: it will rain. If the moon becomes visible on the 30th day: there will be frost, variant: rumor of an enemy in the land. If the moon at its appearance stands low and becomes visible: a present from a distant land will come to the king of the world. ruling ruling From Rašil the older, servant of the king.
If the moon becomes visible on the 30th day: there will be frost, variant: rumor of the enemy. (SPACER) From Nabû-mušeṣi. (SPACER)

[If the moon becomes visible in] Nisan (I) on the 30th day: [Su]bartu [will devour] the Ahlamû; a foreigner will rule the Westland. ruling We are Subartu. ruling If the moon becomes visible on the 30th day: there will be frost in the land. ruling "Frost" is "cold." ruling The moon will be seen with the sun in Tebet (X) on the 14[th] day. ruling The moon will complete the day in Shebat (XI); on the 14th day it will be seen with the sun. ruling [The moon] will reject the day [in] Adar (XII); [on the xth day] it will be seen with the sun. ruling [The moon] will complete the day in Nisan (I). ruling [From Na]bû-ahhe-eriba.

If the moon becomes visible on the 30th day: there will be frost, variant: rumor of an ememy. If the moon becomes visible in Sivan (III) on the 30th day: the Ahlamû will devour the wealth of the Westland. From Rašil the older, servant of the king.

[If the moon in] Sivan (III) becomes visible on the 30th day: the Ahlamû will devour [the wealth] of the Westland. [If the moon] becomes visible on the 30th day: frost; there will be rumor of the enemy. [If at the moon's] appearance the north wind blows: that month the flood will come. ruling [If at the sun's] rising a cloudbank lies to its right: Adad will devastate. ruling Mars has departed, it goes forward; it will become stationary [in] Scorpius. [To]morrow I shall inform the king my lord. ruling [From] B[alas]î.

If the moon becomes visible in Sivan (III) on the 30th day: the Ahlamû will devour the wealth of the Westland. ruling If the moon is red at its appearance: the land will see plenty; the land will enjoy abundant business. ruling If the moon is red at its appearance, and its left horn is blunt, its right horn pointed: you will drive back the enemy land; Adad will devastate. ruling If the moon at its appearance wears a crown: the king will reach the highest rank. ruling If the moon at its appearance becomes very bright: the harvest of the land will prosper. ruling In Sivan (III) on the 14th day the moon will be seen together with the sun. ruling From Nergal-eṭir.

If the moon becomes visible in Sivan (III) on the 30th day: the Ahlamû will devour the wealth of the Westland. ruling If the moon becomes visible on the 30th day: there will be frost, variant: rumor of the enemy. ruling If the moon at its appearance is visible early: the month will bring worry. ruling 2 months after each other (the moon) rejected the day, Iyyar (II) (and) Sivan (III). ruling If a cloudbank lies to the right of the sun: Adad will devastate, variant: rains and floods will come. ruling Mars has turned around, started moving, and is going forward in Scorpius; that is a bad sign. ruling Let them finish the muster quickly. The going out of the king my lord should be rare, until we see how (Mars) moves and stands. ruling From Nabû-ahhe-eriba.
If the moon becomes visible in Ab (V) on the [30]th day: dispersal of Akkad. If the moon becomes visible on the 30th day: there will be frost, variant: rumor of the enemy. [From] Urad-Ea.

If the moon becom[es visible] on the 30th day: there will be frost, variant: rumor of the enemy. If (the moon) becomes visible in Kislev (IX) on the 30th day: the king of the Westland will fall in battle. The Westland [......; the] sign concerns th[em]. Should it be re[pea]ted (in Tebet), it would [concern] us. From the Chief Scribe.
If the moon becomes visible in Kislev (IX) on the 30th day: the king of the Westland will fall in battle. ruling (SPACER) From Nabû-ahhe-eriba.

If the moon becomes visible in Tebet (X) on the 30th day: the Ahlamû will devour Subartu; a foreigner will rule the Westland. ruling Tebet (X) means Elam. From Bulluṭu. (SPACER) We watched on the 29th day; the clouds were den[se], we did not see the moon. We watched on the 30th day; we saw the moon, (but) it was (already) very high. The (weather) of the 29th day has to do with it. What is it that the king my lord says? ruling

If in Tebet (X) the moon becomes visible on the 30th day: the Ahlamû will devour Subartu; a foreigner will rule the Westland. If the moon is low at its appearance: the products of a distant land will come to the king of the world. From Bulluṭu. (SPACER)
If the moon becomes visible in Tebet (X) on the 30th day: the Ahlamû will devour Subartu; a foreigner will rule the Westland. (SPACER) From Šumaya.

If the moon [becomes visible] on the 30th day: there will be frost, variant: rumor of the enemy. If the moon becomes visible in Shebat (XI) on the 30th day: an eclipse of all lands will take place. From the Chief Scribe. (SPACER)

(Beginning destroyed) [If ......] becomes visible: [......] ... ruling [If the moon] becomes visible [in Sivan (III)] on the 30th day: [the Ahlamû] will devour [the wealth of the West]land. (Rest destroyed) (SPACER)
[If the moon is seen in Elul (VI) on the 30th day: dispersal] of Su[bartu]. [If the moon] is seen on the 30th [day]: [there will b]e frost; variant: rumor of the enemy. From Bamaya. (SPACER)

[If the moon] becomes visible [on the 30th day: there will be frost; varia]nt: rumor of the enemy. ruling [...] disease. ruling [...] its left [horn ......]. (Break) [......] ruling (SPACER) [From Nabû-ahh]e-eriba.

(Beginning destroyed) If the moo[n and sun are in opposition]: the king of the la[nd will widen his understanding]. If the moon [and sun are in balance]: the king [of the world will make] the thro[ne last long]. ruling ... [......]. (Rest destroyed)

If the moon [is ...] in its course: [there will be] evil. ruling — It [will be seen] on the 13th day (with the sun). ruling 4 months after ea[ch other] (the moon) completed the day; it rose high in [...], on the 13th day [it was seen with the sun]. The king my lord need not [be afraid] of the bad omen. ruling If the moon at its appearance "pile[s up]" the sky: it will rain, variant: the floo[d will come]. ruling [— It became visible in a piled-up cl]oud. ruling If [the Anzu star ...] is bright: either [frost] or cold [...]. ruling The Anzu star [is Mars]. ruling "Frost" [is "cold"]. ruling If the strange star [comes close to] Gemini: [the ruler will die]. ruling "The ruler will die," as it says, [refers] to [...]. From Nabû-ahhe-eriba.

[Observation] of the moon which was seen: its [posi]tion is stable; it was seen inside a black cloud. ruling [If] the moon becomes visible in Sivan (III) on the 30th day: the Ahlamû will devour the [wealt]h of the Westland; [according to] another [tabl]et: dispersal of all lands. [If the moon's] horns [at its appearance] are very dark: [disbanding] of the fortified outposts, [retiring of the guards]; there will be [recon]ciliation [and peace in the land]. ruling [...] gi is "to be dark"; [gi] is "to be peaceful." — It [stan]ds in a stable position; variant: It was seen in a black cloud. ruling From Akkullanu.

[If the moon] becomes late at an inappropriate time and does not become visible: attack of a ruling city. ruling — It sets on the 15th day and is seen with the sun on the 16th day. ruling If Mars keeps going around a planet: barley will become expensive. If the Raven star reaches the path of the sun: business will diminish; variant: there will be clamor. ruling These are words concerning Akkad. Mars remained four fingers distant from Saturn, it did not come close. It did not reach it. I have (nevertheless) copied (the relevant omen). What does it matter? Let the pertinent namburbi ritual be performed. ruling If the moon is seen on the 16th day: the king of Subartu will become strong and have no opponent. ruling If the moon is surrounded by a halo and a planet stands in it: robbers will rage. If the moon is surrounded by a halo and Mars stands in it: destruction of cattle and wild animals; the kor-measure will decrease; date plantations will not prosper; the Westland will become small. If the moon is surrounded by a halo and the Field star stands in it: reduction of barley. ruling The Field star (means) Virgo. From Balasî. Mars will go away from this (point), it is [in f]ront of Saturn. [The moon] will complete the day in Nisan (I).

Observation of the moon which [was seen] on the 16th day together [with the sun]. The 16th day (refers to) Subartu ... [......]; but in truth, [it is] a bad omen for Akkad. ruling If the moon beco[mes late] at an inappropriate time [and is not seen: attack of a ruling city]. If the moon [is not seen] in Adar (XII) either on the 14th or [on the 15th day ...] Ur [......]. If on the 16th day the moon and sun [are seen together: the king will be shut up] in his palace for the length [of a month; the step of the enemy will be set towards his land; the ene]my will [march around] victoriously in his land. [If the moon ......] is seen and [......]. ruling [...] someone [...... which] surrounded [......] did not surround [......]. ruling [......] and is decided [......]. ruling [...... mo]rning watch [......]. ruling [If ...] the moon lies [......] ... If [.....] thunders [......]. ruling As to Mars which [moved] towards [Saturn], it will absolutely not come close [......]. ruling A planet flared up and surrounded the sun [......]. ruling Nigin is "to surround," ni[gin is ...]. ruling Concerning that it cam[e near] in front of it, I have [already] sent its explanation to the king my lord [......]. ruling Concerning the burnt offerings about which the king wr[ote to me], I opened [my hands] and prayed for the king, the crown prince, and Šamaš-[šumu-ukin. ......]. [......] of the moon are very good [......] a one-year old ... [...] and oil, honey, aromatics; let the king ask [...]naya. From Akkullanu.

If the moon is surrounded by a halo, and Šulpa'e [stands] in [it]: attack of the troops of the Westla[nd]; variant: there will be barley so that there will be no famine; variant: the king of Akkad will experience confinement. ruling If the moon is surrounded by a river: there will be a green crop in the land; variant: it will rain. ruling — A halo surrounded Erua. ruling If the star of Marduk produces a mišhu: the land will eat abundant bread. ruling — The star of Marduk, Mercury, is going beyond its (normal) position and ascends. ruling Afterwards, on the 14th day, (the moon) [was seen] with the s[un]. From Bala[sî].

If the moon is surrounded by a halo, and Jupiter stands in it: the king of Akkad will be shut up. If the moon is surrounded by a halo, and Cancer stands in it: the king of Akkad will extend the life. (SPACER) From Issar-šumu-ereš.

This night, the moon was surrounded by a halo, [and] Jupiter and Scorpius [stood] in [it]. ruling If the moon is surrounded by a halo, and Sagmegar (Jupiter) stands in it: the king of Akkad will be shut up. If the moon is surrounded by a halo, and Nēberu stands in it: fall of cattle and wild animals. The star of Marduk at its appearance is (called) Šulpa'e; when it rises 1 'double-hour' it is (called) Sagmegar; when it stands in the middle of the sky, it is (called) Nēberu. If the moon is surrounded by a halo, and Scorpius stands in it: entu-priestesses will be made pregnant; men, variant: lions, will rage and block the traffic of the land. These (omens) are from the series. If the moon is surrounded by a halo, and Šulpa'e stands in it: the king of the Westland will exercise supreme power and bring about the defeat of his enemy's land. This (omen) is extraneous. From Nabû-mušeṣi.
The night of the 2nd day, Jupiter stood in the halo of the moon. ruling Let them perform a namburbi ritual; the halo was not a closed one. (SPACER) From Nabû-ahhe-eriba.

If the moon [is surrounded] by a halo and Jupiter [stands] in [it]: the king of Akkad will be sh[ut up]. If the moon is surrou[nded] by a halo, and Regulus [stands] in it: in this year women [will give birth to male children]. (Rest destroyed)
If Jupiter [in Sivan (III) stands] in the halo of the moon [...]. ruling A halo of Sivan (III) [...] the king ... [......]. (Break) (SPACER) the king to [......]. ruling (SPACER)

If the moon is surrounded by a black halo: the month holds rain, variant: clouds will be gathered. ruling — Saturn stands in the halo of the moon. ruling If the moon is surrounded by a halo, and Mars stands in it: loss of cattle; the Westland will become smaller. ruling That is bad for the Westland. ruling If the moon is surrounded by a halo, and Regulus stands in it: in that year pregnant women [wil]l give birth to male children. ruling — Re[gulus] stands in the halo of the moon. ruling [From Nabû]-ahhe-eriba.

If [on the 16th] day [the moon and sun] are seen [together: one king] will send [hostile messages to another]; the king will be shut up [in his palace for the length of a month]; the step [of the enemy] will be set [towards] his [land]; the enemy will march around [victoriously in his land]. ruling If the moon is surrounded by a halo and the sun stands in the halo of the moon: they will speak the truth in all lands; the son will speak the truth with his father; universal peace. If the moon is surrounded by a halo and Mars stands in it: loss of cattle in all lands; the date plantation will not prosper; variant: the Westland will diminish. If the moon is surrounded by a halo and two stars stand in the halo of the moon: a reign of long days. ruling If Mars and a planet confront each other and stand there: attack of Elam. If Mars stands [...]: the king of Elam will die. ruling From B[ama]ya.

If the moon is surrounded by a halo, and Sa[turn] stands in it: there [will b]e truth in the land, and the son will speak the truth with his father; universal peace. If ditto Regulus stands in it: women will give birth to male children. [If ditt]o Mars stands in it: fall of cattle in all lands; the plantation of dates and sesame will not prosper, variant: the Westland will diminish. (SPACER) From Urad-Ea.

[If] the moon is surrounded by a halo and Mars [stands] in it: [there will be] loss of cattle in all lands; the date plantation will not prosper; variant: the Westland will diminish. If the sun stands in the halo of the moon: in all lands (people) will speak the truth; the son will sp[eak] the truth with his father. If two stars st[and] in the halo of the moon: reigns of lon[g] days. From Bamaya. (SPACER)

[If the moon] is seen on the [1]6th [day: the king of Subartu will become] strong and [have no rival]. ruling [If] the moon is surrounded by a halo, and Mars [stands in it]: loss of cattle of all lands; the date plantation will not prosper; variant: the Westland will be diminished. ruling [If] the moon is surrounded by a halo, and a planet stands in it: robbers will rage. ruling — Saturn stands in the halo of the moon. ruling If Jupiter comes near to the Bull of Heaven: the treasures of the land will perish; variant: the offspring of large and small cattle will not prosper. ruling Jupiter entered the Bull of Heaven; the king my lord should beware of drafts. ruling If the Plough star reaches the path of the sun: famine of cattle; there will be famine. ruling — Mars will reach Saturn. ruling If a planet and Mars confront each other and stand there: attack of the enemy. ruling If the rising of the Fox star is [...] dark, its star keeps turning like ... [......]. From [Nabû]-ahhe-[eriba].

If in Kislev (IX) [Scorpius'] breast is bright, [its tail is dark], its horns are opposing each other: rain and flood [will be early] for the la[nd; locusts] will attack and devour the land; fall of cattle [and shepherds' huts]; weapons will be raised and [the enemy land will ...], variant: will be conquered [......]. ruling Mercury and [......]. ruling If the moon is surrounded by a halo, and [......]: loss of cattle [......]. If ditto and a star stands in it: [the king and the troops] will be shut up [...]. If ditto, and Cancer [stands in it]: the king of Akkad [will extend] the life. Cancer [...]. If ditto, and its gate [is open] towards [...]: for the land ... [......].

If the moon is surrounded by a halo and the star (called) "Crown of Anu" stands in it: [the king] is well; there will be tru[th and justice] in the land. ruling — Taurus stands in the halo of the moon. For two days in a row it stood in the halo of the moon. ruling (SPACER) From Nabû-ahhe-eriba.

This night the moon was surrounded by a halo in Scorpius, (that is a sign) for rain [and] flood. ruling Venus (and) Mercury are about to set. [......] contains [......] cloud [......] he will see [......] Kalizi [......]... Let them [decide] about the orders and write us. The scribe [...-nap]ištu-iddin will come [on the xth] day. [I se]nt Nabû-mušeṣi, the scribe of the temple, [as the k]ing my lord said; he is bringing the former ... From the Chief Scribe.

[The moon] was surrounded by a halo, Cancer [sto]od in it. This is its interpretation: ruling [If the moon] is surrounded by a halo and Cancer stands in it: the king of Akkad will extend the life. [If the moon] is surrounded by a halo and the north wind blows: the gods will raise [..., and] the gods will provide good fortune for the land. [If the moon] is surrounded by [a halo] and it lingers on: givi[ng of the re]ign to [the king]. ruling [If] there is fog [in the land]: plenty [for the people]. [If] there is continually fog [in] the land: the dynasty of the land will rule the world. [If] there is continuous fog every day: business will become abundant. ruling [From] Šumaya.

[If the moon] is surrounded [by a halo] and Cancer stands [in it]: the king of Akkad will extend the life. ruling This [night] (the moon) was surrounded by a halo, it did not close. ruling [If] the moon is surrounded by a drawing: there will be an eclipse. ruling Eclipse (means) trouble. ruling This night (the moon) was surrounded by a drawing, it did not close. ruling If Venus comes close to Scorpius: winds which are not good will blow towards the land; Adad will give his rains, and Ea his springs, to the Gutian land. ruling If (Venus) came close to the Breast of Scorpius, it would be copied like this; now so far she has not approached it; when she will have approached she will not come close, she will pass at some distance. The planets pass like this above the stars which are in their path. ruling [If] the Frond star reaches the Pleiades: Adad will devastate. ruling Mars did not approach the Fish-of-the-Sky [...] ..., it departed from it. ... [......]. ... [......] ... [......].

[This night] the moon was surrounded by a halo; [Canc]er and Gemini stood in it. ruling If the moon is surrounded by a halo and Cancer [stands] in [it]: the king of Akkad will [extend] the life. If the moon is surrounded by a halo and a draw[ing stands in it]: destruction [...]. Drawing (means) G[emini]. From Bulluṭu. (SPACER)

If the moon is surrounded by a river: there will be great floods and cloudbreaks. — The Frond star st[ood] in it. If the moon is surrounded by a halo and the Bo[w star stands in it]: men will rage, and robberies will [become frequent] in the land. From Nabû-muše[ṣi].

(Beginning destroyed) [...] ... [...] ... [......] ... [......] ... ruling [......] let them perform a namburbi ritual. ruling [ If the moon] wears a rainbow [crown: Adad will devastate the land's harvest; it] will rain [......] ... ruling (Rest destroyed)

If the moon is seen at an inappropriate time: business will diminish. ruling — On the 12th day it was seen with the sun. ruling If the moon and sun are seen together not at the normal time: a strong enemy will oppress the land; the king of Akkad will bring about the downfall of his enemy. ruling — [On the 1]2th [day] it was seen with the sun. ruling [If the moon] is seen on the 12th day: bad for Akkad, it is [go]od for Elam and the Westland; ruling This is bad for Akkad. From Balasî.

[If on the 1]3th day the moon [and sun] are seen together: (unreliable speech; there will be bad ways in the land); [the step of the enemy will be in the land]; the enemy will [plunder] in the land. (SPACER) ruling (Rest destroyed) (SPACER)

If the moon reaches the sun and follows it closely, and one horn meets the other: there will be truth in the land, and the son will speak the truth with his father. — On the 14th day one god will be seen with the other. If the moon and sun follow each other closely: the king of the land will widen his understanding; the foundation of the throne of the king of the land will become stable. — On the 14th day one god will be seen with the other. (If) on the 14th day the moon and sun are seen together: the speech of the land will become reliable; the land will become happy; the gods will remember Akkad favorably; joy among the troops; the cattle of Akkad will lie in the steppe undisturbed. If the moon is surrounded by a black halo: clouds will be gathered; this month holds rain. If one horn meets the other: the flood will come. — On the 14th day one god is seen with the other. ruling Concerning the people about whom I wrote to the king my lord, the king must not say as follows: "Why did he not say, 'Let them lead the people here!'" The king knows that I have no field in Assyria. What shall I eat and what shall I give to them? Who is my god, who is my lord, at whom are my eyes set except the king my lord, for whose life I pray to Šamaš. While Ahhešâ is here, let the king write to him, and let his messenger lead the people away so that I can meet (them) in Babylon. Let him lead my son Nabû-eṭir-napšati, a servant of the king, here so that he can ser[ve] the king together with me!
If on the 14th day the moon and sun are seen together: reliable speech, the land will become happy; the gods will remember Akkad favorably. ruling From Ahhešâ of Uruk. (SPACER)

If the moon reaches the sun and follows it closely, (and) one horn meets the other: there will be truth in the land, and the son will speak the truth with his father; universal peace. If on the 14th day the moon and sun are seen together: reliable speech; happiness; the gods will remember Akkad favorably; joy among the troops; the king will become happy; the cattle of Akkad will lie in the steppe undisturbed. If the moon and sun are in balance: the land will become stable; reliable speech will be placed in the mouth of people; the king will make his throne last long. If the moon and sun are in opposition: the king of the land will widen his understanding. From Bamaya.

If the moon and sun are in balance: the land will become stable; reliable speech will be placed in the mouth of people; the king of the land will make his throne last long. If the moon and sun are in opposition: the king of the land will widen his understanding. If on the 14th day the moon and sun are seen together: reliable speech, the land will become happy. The gods will remember Akkad favorably; joy among the troops; the king will become happy; the cattle of Akkad will lie in the steppe undisturbed. (SPACER) From Issar-šumu-ereš.

[If] on the 14th day the moon and sun are seen together: reliable speech, the land will become happy; [the god]s will remember Akkad favorably; joy among the troops; the king will become happy; the cattle of Akkad will lie in the steppe undisturbed. If the moon and sun are in balance: the land will bec[ome stable]; reliable speech will be placed in the mouth of people; the king of the land will make his throne last long. (SPACER) [From Is]sar-šumu-ereš.

If on the 14th day the moon and sun are seen together: reliable speech; the land will become happy; the gods will remember Akkad favorably; [the cattle] of Akkad will lie in the steppe undisturbed; [joy among the troo]ps; the king will become happy. [......] ... (Break) (SPACER) [disb]anding of the fortified outposts, retiring of the gu[ard]s; there will be reconciliation and peace in the land. ruling

[If the moon and sun] are in balance: the land will become stable: reliable [speech] will be placed [in the mouth] of people; [the king] of the land will make his throne last long. [If the moon] and sun are in opposition: the king of the land will widen his understanding. If on the 14th day the moon and sun are seen together: reliable speech; the land will become happy; the gods will remember Akkad favorably; joy among the troops; the king will become happy; the cattle of Akkad will lie in the steppe undisturbed. (SPACER) From Bulluṭu.

If on the 14th day the moon and su[n] are seen together: reliable speech; the land will become happy; the gods will re[memb]er Akkad favorably; the cattle of Akkad will lie in the steppe undisturbed; joy among the troops; the king will become happy. [If the moon and sun are in o]pposition: there will be a truth in the land, and the son will speak the truth with his father. If the day reaches its normal length: a reign of long days. From Adad-šumu-uṣur.

[If] on the 14th [day] the moon and sun [are seen] together: reliable speech, the land will become happy; the gods will remember Akkad [favorably]; joy among the troops; [the king will become happy]; the cattle of Akkad wil[l lie] in the steppe undisturbed. If the moon and sun are in balance: the land will become stable; reliable speech [will be placed] in the m[outh of people]; the king of the land will make the throne last long. If the moon reaches the sun and follows it closely, and one horn me[ets] the other: there will be truth [in] the land, and the son will [speak] the truth with his father. If the moon and sun are in opposition: the king of the land will widen his understanding. ruling — On the 14th day it will be seen with the sun. ruling If on the 14th day the moon and sun are seen together: reliable speech; the land will become happy; the gods will reme[mber] Akkad favorab[ly]; the cattle of Akkad will lie [in the steppe undisturbed]. ruling [...] on the 14th day [the moon and] sun were seen to[gether]; its interpretation is like t[his]: ruling If the moon and [sun are] in balance: reliable spee[ch] will be placed in the mouth of people; the king of the universe will make the throne last long. ruling Tishri (VII), 14th day, eponymy of [La]basi.

If the moon and sun are in opposition: the king [of the land] will widen [his understanding]. If the moon [and sun are in bal]ance: [the land will become stable]; reliable [speech] will be placed [in the mouth of people; the king of the land will make his throne] last long. If [the moon and sun] are seen together [on the 14th day: reliable] sp[eech; the land will become] happy; the gods will remember Akkad favorably; joy among the troops; the king will become happy; the cattle of Akkad will lie in the steppe undisturbed. From Nabû-mušeṣi.

[If on the 14th day the moon and sun are seen] together: reli[able speech]; the land will become happy; [the gods] will remember [Akkad] favorab[ly; joy among] the troops; [the king will become happy; the cattle] of Akkad will l[ie in the steppe] undisturbed. ruling [If the moon and] sun are in opposition: [the king] of the land will wid[en his un]derstanding. ruling — On the 14th [day] the moon will be seen [with] the sun. ruling [If] the moon and [sun are] in balance: relia[ble sp]eech will be placed [in the mouth] of people; [the king] of the land will make the [thr]one last long. ruling

[If on the 14th day the moon and sun are see]n [together: reliable speech]; the land will become [ha]ppy; [the gods] will remember [Akkad favorably; joy among the troops]; the king will become happy; [the cattle of Akkad] will lie [in the steppe undis]turbed. [If the moon reaches the sun and follows it closely, one horn] meets the other: [there will be truth in the land, and the son] will speak the truth with his [father ......] will be made entirely.
